Styling

Complete documentation for cell formatting and styling in Aspose.Cells FOSS for .NET

← Back to Index

Style Overview

The Style class represents the complete set of formatting properties that can be applied to cells in a worksheet. Styles control how cells look and behave, including fonts, colors, borders, alignment, number formats, and protection.

Key Style Components:
  • Font: Font family, size, weight, color, and effects
  • Borders: Line style, color, and weight for all four sides
  • Fill: Pattern type, foreground color, and background color
  • Alignment: Horizontal and vertical alignment, text wrapping, and indentation
  • Number Format: Built-in or custom number format strings
  • Protection: Locked and hidden states

Getting and Setting Styles

Basic Style Operations
// Get style from a cell
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Modify style properties
style.Font.Bold = true;
style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
style.BackgroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Yellow;

// Apply style back to cell
cell.SetStyle(style);

// Combine into one line
cell.GetStyle().Font.Bold = true;
cell.SetStyle(style);
Style Best Practices
// GOOD: Get style once, modify, then apply
var style = cell.GetStyle();
style.Font.Bold = true;
style.Font.Size = 12;
style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.Blue;
cell.SetStyle(style);

// AVOID: Multiple Get/Set calls
cell.GetStyle().Font.Bold = true;      // Inefficient
cell.GetStyle().Font.Size = 12;        // Creates new style each time
cell.GetStyle().Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.Blue;
cell.SetStyle(cell.GetStyle());        // Multiple style creations
Reusing Styles
// Create a header style once
var headerStyle = workbook.CreateStyle();
headerStyle.Font.Bold = true;
headerStyle.Font.Size = 14;
headerStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
headerStyle.BackgroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.FromArgb(0, 51, 153);
headerStyle.HorizontalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Center;

// Apply to multiple cells
for (int col = 0; col < 5; col++)
{
    worksheet.Cells[0, col].SetStyle(headerStyle);
}

Font Formatting

Basic Font Properties
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Font name and size
style.Font.Name = "Arial";
style.Font.Size = 12;

// Font weight
style.Font.Bold = true;
style.Font.Bold = false;

// Font style
style.Font.Italic = true;
style.Font.Italic = false;

// Font underline
style.Font.Underline = FontUnderlineType.Single;
style.Font.Underline = FontUnderlineType.Double;
style.Font.Underline = FontUnderlineType.None;

// Strikeout
style.Font.Strikeout = true;

// Font color
style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.FromArgb(255, 0, 0);

cell.SetStyle(style);
Font Examples
// Bold red header
var boldRedStyle = cell.GetStyle();
boldRedStyle.Font.Bold = true;
boldRedStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
boldRedStyle.Font.Size = 14;

// Italic blue text
var italicBlueStyle = cell.GetStyle();
italicBlueStyle.Font.Italic = true;
italicBlueStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.Blue;

// Underlined green text
var underlinedStyle = cell.GetStyle();
underlinedStyle.Font.Underline = FontUnderlineType.Single;
underlinedStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.Green;

// Strikeout gray text
var strikeoutStyle = cell.GetStyle();
strikeoutStyle.Font.Strikeout = true;
strikeoutStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.Gray;

Borders

Border Properties
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Border line types
style.Borders[BorderType.LeftBorder].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thin;
style.Borders[BorderType.RightBorder].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thin;
style.Borders[BorderType.TopBorder].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thin;
style.Borders[BorderType.BottomBorder].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thin;

// Border line styles available:
// - None
// - Thin
// - Medium
// - Dashed
// - Dotted
// - Thick
// - Double
// - Hair
// - MediumDashed
// - DashDot
// - MediumDashDot
// - DashDotDot
// - MediumDashDotDot
// - SlantedDashDot

// Border colors
style.Borders[BorderType.LeftBorder].Color = System.Drawing.Color.Black;
style.Borders[BorderType.RightBorder].Color = System.Drawing.Color.Black;
style.Borders[BorderType.TopBorder].Color = System.Drawing.Color.Black;
style.Borders[BorderType.BottomBorder].Color = System.Drawing.Color.Black;

cell.SetStyle(style);
Border Patterns
// All borders thin
var allBordersStyle = cell.GetStyle();
foreach (BorderType border in new[] { BorderType.LeftBorder, BorderType.RightBorder,
                                        BorderType.TopBorder, BorderType.BottomBorder })
{
    allBordersStyle.Borders[border].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thin;
    allBordersStyle.Borders[border].Color = System.Drawing.Color.Black;
}

// Thick border outline
var thickOutlineStyle = cell.GetStyle();
thickOutlineStyle.Borders[BorderType.LeftBorder].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thick;
thickOutlineStyle.Borders[BorderType.RightBorder].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thick;
thickOutlineStyle.Borders[BorderType.TopBorder].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thick;
thickOutlineStyle.Borders[BorderType.BottomBorder].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thick;

// Dashed border
var dashedStyle = cell.GetStyle();
dashedStyle.Borders[BorderType.AllBorders].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Dashed;
dashedStyle.Borders[BorderType.AllBorders].Color = System.Drawing.Color.Blue;

Fill and Background

Solid Fill
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Set solid pattern
style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

// Set background color (used with Solid pattern)
style.BackgroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Yellow;
style.BackgroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.FromArgb(255, 255, 0);

// Alternative approach
style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Yellow;

cell.SetStyle(style);
Pattern Fill
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Pattern types available:
// - None
// - Solid
// - Gray50
// - Gray75
// - Gray125
// - Gray25
// - HorizontalStripe
// - VerticalStripe
// - ReverseDiagonalStripe
// - DiagonalStripe
// - DiagonalCrosshatch
// - ThickDiagonalCrosshatch
// - ThinHorizontalStripe
// - ThinVerticalStripe
// - ThinReverseDiagonalStripe
// - ThinDiagonalStripe
// - ThinHorizontalCrosshatch
// - ThinDiagonalCrosshatch
// - 12.5% Gray
// - 6.25% Gray

// Set pattern and colors
style.Pattern = BackgroundType.HorizontalStripe;
style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Blue;   // Foreground color
style.BackgroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.White;  // Background color

cell.SetStyle(style);

Alignment

Horizontal Alignment
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Horizontal alignment options:
// - General
// - Left
// - Center
// - Right
// - Fill
// - Justify
// - CenterAcross
// - Distributed

style.HorizontalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Center;
style.HorizontalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Left;
style.HorizontalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Right;

cell.SetStyle(style);
Vertical Alignment
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Vertical alignment options:
// - Top
// - Center
// - Bottom
// - Justify
// - Distributed

style.VerticalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Center;
style.VerticalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Top;

cell.SetStyle(style);
Text Wrapping and Indentation
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Enable text wrapping
style.WrapText = true;
style.WrapText = false;

// Text indentation (in character widths)
style.IndentLevel = 1;
style.IndentLevel = 2;
style.IndentLevel = 0;  // No indentation

// Text rotation (degrees)
style.RotationAngle = 45;    // 45 degrees clockwise
style.RotationAngle = -45;   // 45 degrees counter-clockwise
style.RotationAngle = 90;    // 90 degrees

cell.SetStyle(style);

Number Formatting

Built-in Number Formats
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Built-in format index
style.Number = 0;      // General
style.Number = 1;      // 0
style.Number = 2;      // 0.00
style.Number = 3;      // #,##0
style.Number = 4;      // #,##0.00
style.Number = 5;      // $#,##0;$#,##0
style.Number = 6;      // $#,##0;[Red]$#,##0
style.Number = 7;      // $#,##0.00;$#,##0.00
style.Number = 8;      // $#,##0.00;[Red]$#,##0.00
style.Number = 9;      // 0%
style.Number = 10;     // 0.00%
style.Number = 11;     // 0.00E+00
style.Number = 12;     // # ?/?
style.Number = 13;     # ??/??
style.Number = 14;     // m/d/yy
style.Number = 15;     // d-mmm-yy
style.Number = 16;     // d-mmm
style.Number = 17;     // mmm-yy
style.Number = 18;     // h:mm AM/PM
style.Number = 19;     // h:mm:ss AM/PM
style.Number = 20;     // h:mm
style.Number = 21;     // h:mm:ss
style.Number = 22;     // m/d/yy h:mm
style.Number = 37;     // #,##0 ;(#,##0)
style.Number = 38;     // #,##0 ;[Red](#,##0)
style.Number = 39;     // #,##0.00;(#,##0.00)
style.Number = 40;     // #,##0.00;[Red](#,##0.00)
style.Number = 44;     // _,(* #,##0.00_);_(* (#,##0.00);_(* "-"_);_(@_)

cell.SetStyle(style);
Custom Number Formats
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Currency format
style.Custom = "$#,##0.00";
style.Custom = "€#,##0.00";

// Percentage format
style.Custom = "0.00%";
style.Custom = "0.0%";

// Date format
style.Custom = "yyyy-MM-dd";
style.Custom = "MM/dd/yyyy";
style.Custom = "dd-MMM-yyyy";

// Time format
style.Custom = "hh:mm:ss";
style.Custom = "HH:mm";

// Phone number format
style.Custom = "(###) ###-####";

// Scientific notation
style.Custom = "0.00E+00";

// Custom text with conditions
style.Custom = "[>1000]\"Large\";[>100]\"Medium\";\"Small\"";

cell.SetStyle(style);
NumberFormat Property
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Alternative way to set number format
style.NumberFormat = "$#,##0.00";
style.NumberFormat = "0.00%";
style.NumberFormat = "yyyy-MM-dd";

cell.SetStyle(style);

Protection

Cell Protection Settings
var style = cell.GetStyle();

// Lock cell (default is true)
style.IsLocked = true;   // Cell is locked when worksheet is protected
style.IsLocked = false;  // Cell can be edited when worksheet is protected

// Hide formula (default is false)
style.IsHidden = true;   // Formula is hidden when worksheet is protected
style.IsHidden = false;  // Formula is visible

cell.SetStyle(style);

// IMPORTANT: Cell protection only works when worksheet is protected
worksheet.Protect("password");
Protection Example
// Create a form with protected cells and editable cells
var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Set up header row (protected, read-only)
for (int col = 0; col < 3; col++)
{
    var cell = worksheet.Cells[0, col];
    cell.PutValue($"Column {col + 1}");

    var style = cell.GetStyle();
    style.Font.Bold = true;
    style.IsLocked = true;  // Read-only
    cell.SetStyle(style);
}

// Set up data entry area (unlocked, editable)
for (int row = 1; row <= 5; row++)
{
    for (int col = 0; col < 3; col++)
    {
        var cell = worksheet.Cells[row, col];
        var style = cell.GetStyle();
        style.IsLocked = false;  // Editable
        cell.SetStyle(style);
    }
}

// Protect the worksheet
worksheet.Protect("form_password");

Complete Example

Create Styled Report
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var workbook = new Workbook();
        var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
        var cells = worksheet.Cells;

        // Create header style
        var headerStyle = workbook.CreateStyle();
        headerStyle.Font.Bold = true;
        headerStyle.Font.Size = 14;
        headerStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        headerStyle.BackgroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.FromArgb(0, 51, 153);
        headerStyle.HorizontalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Center;
        headerStyle.VerticalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Center;
        headerStyle.Borders[BorderType.AllBorders].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thin;
        headerStyle.Borders[BorderType.AllBorders].Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;

        // Create data style
        var dataStyle = workbook.CreateStyle();
        dataStyle.Font.Size = 11;
        dataStyle.HorizontalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Center;
        dataStyle.VerticalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Center;
        dataStyle.Borders[BorderType.AllBorders].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thin;
        dataStyle.Borders[BorderType.AllBorders].Color = System.Drawing.Color.Black;

        // Create currency style
        var currencyStyle = workbook.CreateStyle();
        currencyStyle.Font.Size = 11;
        currencyStyle.HorizontalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Right;
        currencyStyle.VerticalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Center;
        currencyStyle.NumberFormat = "$#,##0.00";
        currencyStyle.Borders[BorderType.AllBorders].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Thin;
        currencyStyle.Borders[BorderType.AllBorders].Color = System.Drawing.Color.Black;

        // Create total style
        var totalStyle = workbook.CreateStyle();
        totalStyle.Font.Bold = true;
        totalStyle.Font.Size = 12;
        totalStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        totalStyle.BackgroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.FromArgb(0, 102, 204);
        totalStyle.HorizontalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Right;
        totalStyle.VerticalAlignment = TextAlignmentType.Center;
        totalStyle.NumberFormat = "$#,##0.00";
        totalStyle.Borders[BorderType.AllBorders].LineStyle = CellBorderType.Medium;
        totalStyle.Borders[BorderType.AllBorders].Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;

        // Apply header style
        cells["A1"].PutValue("Product");
        cells["B1"].PutValue("Quantity");
        cells["C1"].PutValue("Unit Price");
        cells["D1"].PutValue("Total");

        for (int col = 0; col <= 3; col++)
        {
            cells[0, col].SetStyle(headerStyle);
        }

        // Add sample data
        string[] products = { "Widget A", "Widget B", "Widget C", "Widget D" };
        int[] quantities = { 100, 150, 200, 75 };
        double[] prices = { 10.50, 15.75, 8.99, 25.00 };

        for (int i = 0; i < products.Length; i++)
        {
            int row = i + 2;

            cells[row, 0].PutValue(products[i]);
            cells[row, 0].SetStyle(dataStyle);

            cells[row, 1].PutValue(quantities[i]);
            cells[row, 1].SetStyle(dataStyle);

            cells[row, 2].PutValue(prices[i]);
            cells[row, 2].SetStyle(currencyStyle);

            // Calculate total
            cells[row, 3].Formula = $"=B{row}*C{row}";
            cells[row, 3].SetStyle(currencyStyle);
        }

        // Add total row
        int totalRow = products.Length + 3;
        cells[totalRow, 2].PutValue("Grand Total:");
        cells[totalRow, 2].SetStyle(totalStyle);

        cells[totalRow, 3].Formula = $"=SUM(D2:D{totalRow - 1})";
        cells[totalRow, 3].SetStyle(totalStyle);

        // Auto-fit columns
        worksheet.AutoFitColumns();

        // Save workbook
        workbook.Save("styled-report.xlsx");
        Console.WriteLine("Styled report created successfully!");
    }
}
Conditional Formatting Example
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var workbook = new Workbook();
        var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
        var cells = worksheet.Cells;

        // Add sample scores
        for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++)
        {
            cells[i, 0].PutValue(new Random().Next(50, 100));
        }

        // Create conditional formatting
        var condition = new FormatCondition();
        condition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
        condition.Operator = OperatorType.Greater;
        condition.Formula1 = "80";
        condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Green;
        condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.Area("A1:A10"), condition);

        workbook.Save("conditional-format.xlsx");
        Console.WriteLine("Conditional formatting applied!");
    }
}

Important Notes

  • Style Efficiency: Get the style once, modify all properties, then set it back to minimize style object creation.
  • Style Reuse: Create and reuse style objects when applying the same formatting to multiple cells.
  • Number Formats: Use built-in formats when possible for better compatibility; use custom formats for specific requirements.
  • Protection: Cell protection only takes effect when the worksheet is protected.
  • Borders: Use BorderType.AllBorders for uniform borders on all sides.
  • Colors: Use System.Drawing.Color for all color specifications in styles.
  • Date Formatting: Cells containing DateTime values require appropriate number formats to display correctly.

Related Documentation

  • Worksheet Operations - Managing worksheets
